meaning: n.裂片,尖片,小事,碎片v.裂成碎片,分裂
examples: The mirror cracked but did not splinter.
"It is a mahogany splinter, "said the other.
He ran a splinter into his finger.
During his 20 years at Intel Splinter saw this law deliver exponentially better products and profits.
"This is like the 1970s in the computer chip business, " says Splinter, flashing a 200-watt smile.
